limitations: Operator chat timing inside this slice is approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
The 0.6.22 package report proves build inventory only; it records distributed:false and does not establish live prerelease authority.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator explicitly stopped the forward drift: if the last versions did not work, the lane should back off to the last known working hosted seam instead of continuing in the same direction.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Selected the last hosted-working seam as the rollback source and restored the 0.6.14 player core instead of layering more fixes onto the broken later line.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.22 cleanly from the restored 0.6.14 hosted-working player core.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Regenerated the 0.6.22 package inventory with -SkipVarDistribute and confirmed it remains build-only with distributed:false.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Redeployed the raw modern_tv hosted-player proof as the canonical 0.6.22 alpha dev_deploy seam on top of the restored 0.6.14 player core.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Refreshed the baseline direct-player raw asset from the validated 0.6.22 release output so the generic player and the modern_tv proof share the same rollback core.

decisions: 0.6.22 is a rollback recovery slice that restores the 0.6.14 hosted-working player core under a fresh semantic version.
The live authority seam for this slice remains raw dev_deploy Custom artifacts, not the .var package.
If a playlist fix is still needed after the rollback, it must be reintroduced narrowly and intentionally rather than inherited from the later drifted line.
openQuestions: Does 0.6.22 restore the boring hosted behavior for load, next, scrub, and movie playback?
If playlists still need help after the rollback, which single playlist-truth seam should be reintroduced without carrying later drift forward?
notes: This slice intentionally backs off the player core to the last hosted-working state instead of trying to preserve the later experimental runtime changes.
The 0.6.22 package report exists because the hosted proof wrapper expects package inventory, and it remains build-only with distributed:false.
